what_it_does: A Node.js end-to-end test script that exercises the episodic-memory plugin against the real Claude Code CLI. It creates temporary directories, seeds a Claude conversation with a unique marker, triggers archiving/summarization/indexing, then asks Claude to recall the marker through the episodic-memory MCP search tool and asserts the result.
observations: Gated by the environment variable EPISODIC_MEMORY_RUN_CLAUDE_E2E=1 because it uses real Claude Code auth.
Resolves the highest-semver `claude` binary on PATH, falling back to `claude` or CLAUDE_BIN env override.
Runs `claude plugin validate` and uses `--dangerously-skip-permissions` during test prompts.
Copies the seed session transcript from ~/.claude/projects into a configured TEST_PROJECTS_DIR source directory.
Polls for up to 180 seconds waiting for the marker to appear in the archive, summaries to be written, and the SQLite index database to exist.
Verifies recall by checking both the result string and the presence of the MCP tool name in Claude's JSON output.
standouts: Full live-CLI integration test rather than mocked unit tests.
Uses a unique timestamped marker as a probe traceable through archive, summary, database, and recall.
Validates the actual MCP tool invocation by inspecting raw Claude JSON output.
conventions: ES module with shebang and top-level await in main().
Sync subprocess calls via spawnSync for setup and assertions, async polling for background indexing.
Heavy use of path.join, fs.realpathSync, and os.tmpdir for portable temp workspace creation.
concerns: Does not clean up the temporary root directory after the test; it is logged and left behind.
Relies on brittle string matching for the MCP tool name in Claude output.
Assumes specific transcript directory layout under ~/.claude/projects and a particular encoded project-dir naming scheme.
Deletes CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR from the environment, which may interfere with normal Claude Code configuration.
notes: A guarded, live-CLI integration harness that probes the episodic-memory pipeline from conversation capture through MCP recall.
questions: What populates conversation-archive and how is the copied transcript discovered by the indexer?
What role does CODEX_HOME play in this test?
Why is CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR explicitly removed from the environment?
Is the temporary directory intentionally preserved for post-mortem debugging?
purpose_vs_actual: The file is clearly an end-to-end validation script and matches that purpose.
intention_signals: To prove that the episodic-memory plugin can capture, archive, summarize, index, and later retrieve a real Claude Code conversation through its MCP tool in an actual CLI session.
novelty: Using a single injected marker as an end-to-end probe that must be traceable through archived JSONL, generated summaries, a SQLite index, and an MCP-driven recall response.
contribution: A self-contained, env-gated integration harness that drives the real Claude Code CLI through the full episodic-memory lifecycle and asserts recall behavior, useful for validating the plugin outside unit tests.
summary_for_embedding: Node.js end-to-end test script that drives the real Claude Code CLI to seed, archive, index, and recall a marker conversation through the episodic-memory plugin.
intention_for_embedding: Provide live, full-stack validation of the episodic-memory plugin's conversation capture, summarization, indexing, and MCP recall capabilities.

symbols.does: Prints an error message prefixed with claude-e2e: and exits the process with code 1.
symbols.description: A small fatal-error helper used to abort the test when preconditions fail, such as the missing env gate or missing build artifact.

symbols.does: Executes a command synchronously with spawnSync and returns combined stdout/stderr, throwing on non-zero exit.
symbols.description: Wraps spawnSync with utf-8 encoding and merged output, centralizing command execution and failure handling for the test harness.

symbols.does: Extracts the first semver-like x.y.z version string from a text output.
symbols.description: Used to compare installed Claude CLI versions by scanning output for a three-part dotted version number.

symbols.does: Compares two semver strings numerically by major, minor, and patch components.
symbols.description: A simple comparator that returns negative, zero, or positive based on the first differing version part, used to pick the newest Claude binary.

symbols.does: Enumerates unique existing files named `name` across every directory in PATH.
symbols.description: Collects candidate binary paths while deduplicating entries, providing the inputs for version-based selection of the Claude CLI.

symbols.does: Chooses the Claude binary to use, preferring CLAUDE_BIN env, then the highest-version `claude` on PATH, then `claude`.
symbols.description: Combines PATH enumeration, version parsing, and semver comparison to pick the most capable available Claude CLI executable.

symbols.does: Returns a promise that resolves after the given number of milliseconds.
symbols.description: A tiny async delay utility used by the polling waitFor helper.

symbols.does: Parses Claude JSON output and returns the message whose type is `result`.
symbols.description: Handles both array and object-shaped Claude responses, throwing if no result message is present.

symbols.does: Encodes a workspace path by replacing forward slashes with dashes.
symbols.description: Mirrors the directory naming convention Claude uses for project transcripts under ~/.claude/projects.

symbols.does: Locates a Claude transcript JSONL file by session id, first under the encoded project dir then recursively.
symbols.description: Provides a fallback recursive search through ~/.claude/projects so the test can find the seed session transcript even if the exact project directory is unknown.

symbols.does: Recursively counts files under a root whose names end with a given suffix.
symbols.description: Used to verify that archived JSONL files and summary files have been produced by the episodic-memory pipeline.

symbols.does: Recursively checks whether any file under a root contains a given string.
symbols.description: A content probe used to wait until the unique marker appears somewhere in the conversation archive.

symbols.does: Polls a predicate every two seconds until it returns truthy or a timeout expires.
symbols.description: Async waiter that gives background indexing and summarization up to three minutes to complete before failing the test.

symbols.does: Invokes the selected Claude CLI with plugin-dir, setting-sources, skip-permissions, and JSON output format in a given workspace.
symbols.description: The main driver for each Claude session in the test, returning both raw output and the parsed result message.

symbols.does: Orchestrates the full e2e flow: gate check, temp dirs, seed session, transcript copy, trigger session, archive/index polling, and recall assertion.
symbols.description: The test entry point that wires together all helpers to validate that a marker conversation can be captured, indexed, and recalled via the episodic-memory MCP tool.
